sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent v0.11.0 "Interface" (Apr 23, traction Apr 25-27) — full React/Ink TUI rewrite, native AWS Bedrock, GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth, 1,556 commits / 761 PRs / 224K insertions / 29 contributors** — Largest Hermes Agent release to date. **Ink-based interactive CLI** (`hermes --tui`) with Python JSON-RPC backend (`tui_gateway`); sticky composer; live streaming with OSC-52 clipboard support; stable picker keys; status bar with per-turn stopwatch + git branch; `/clear` confirm; light-theme preset; subagent-spawn observability overlay. **Pluggable transport architecture under every provider**; 5 new inference paths; 17th messaging platform (QQBot); native AWS Bedrock; GPT-5.5 via Codex OAuth. **Shell hooks** (`pre_tool_call` / `post_tool_call` / `on_session_start`) without Python plugin. **Webhook direct-delivery mode** (zero-LLM push for alerts, uptime, event streams). Subagents with explicit orchestrator role + configurable `max_spawn_depth`; concurrent sibling subagents share filesystem state via file-coordination layer. Auxiliary models per-task overrides screen.
**Hermes Agent v0.11 reference guide — Ink TUI + Bedrock + GPT-5.5 deployment** — Practitioner write-up of the v0.11.0 surface area for production self-hosters.

citations.label: Blake Crosley - Hermes Agent v0.11 Reference: Ink TUI + Bedrock + GPT-5.5

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta train continues into Apr 27 — voice-stack hardening, browser-automation safe-tab URLs, CDP-native role-snapshot fallback, CLI image-gen --background, Discord voice channel model override, plugin-onboarding install-record preservation** — Sub-24h beta cadence persists from Apr 26 13:00 (beta.3) → 13:24 (beta.4) → beta.7+ into Apr 27 working hours. Voice reply now production-shaped with chat-scoped auto-TTS controls + personas + 6 TTS providers (Azure Speech / Xiaomi / Local CLI / Inworld / Volcengine / ElevenLabs v3).

citations.label: newreleases.io - openclaw/openclaw v2026.4.25-beta.4

bullets.body: **NemoClaw — early-preview status carries forward, NVIDIA OpenShell + 5-layer security architecture** — NemoClaw available in early preview from Mar 16, 2026; **5 stacked layers between OpenClaw agent and host**: NVIDIA OpenShell secure runtime as policy-enforcement broker, sandboxed execution, network egress control, minimal-privilege access, privacy routing, intent verification.

citations.label: NVIDIA - Run Autonomous, Self-Evolving Agents More Safely with NVIDIA OpenShell

We will sell you a bomb shelter for $100 million." Comes weeks after Bloomberg confirmed a private Discord forum gained access to Mythos via a third-party Anthropic contractor + URL-pattern guessing.

bullets.body: **CNBC: Public sours on AI and datacenters as Anthropic, OpenAI look to IPO and tech keeps spending (Apr 15, traction Apr 25-27)** — Public-opinion data from the IPO window: rising backlash + physical-attack incidents (Altman's home, Apr 16) frame the cap-ex narrative.

**No major release inside Apr 25-27 — v0.19.1 (Apr 18) carries forward; v0.20 framing (Transformers v5 broad rollout, FA4 default MLA prefill backend, TurboQuant 2-bit KV-cache, Online Quantization Frontend, vLLM IR scaffold) remains anticipated** — vLLM v0.20 is expected to expand Transformers v5 compatibility, push speculative-decoding maturity, and add MoE routing optimizations for Gemma 4 / DeepSeek V3-style models. Same window saw vLLM project ship **v0.19.1** and continue work on Gemma 4 + DeepSeek V3.2 + zero-bubble overlap of speculative decoding × async scheduling.

citations.label: vLLM Blog

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — patch coordination yielded no upstream fix; v0.5.10 ships without SSTI hardening** — Apr 27 status: SGLang 0.5.10 release does not include the recommended `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment` mitigation. **SGLang remains exploitable via crafted GGUF chat_template + Qwen3 reranker trigger phrase against `/v1/rerank`**. PoC at `github.com/Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE` continues to circulate. CERT/CC VU#915947 still active.

citations.label: Cyberpress - Hackers Could Weaponize GGUF Models to Achieve RCE on SGLang Inference Servers

bullets.body: **Same vulnerability class previously patched in vLLM (CVE-2025-61620, CVSS 6.5) late 2025 — copy-paste-vulnerability framing now broadly applies** — Apr 25-27 vendor coverage repeatedly flags this as a **shared-template-rendering primitive** across inference servers, reinforcing the case for an "AI-Infra CVE sub-catalog."

TheCyberThrone covers the matching CISA-KEV catalog disclosure window of 31 high-priority + 19 actively exploited entries.
